title: [FIRRTL][ModuleInliner] Handle inline context properly for HierPath update
body:
This fixes https://github.com/llvm/circt/issues/3630, the crash in `ModuleInliner`. 
`ModuleInliner` was not handling the case when both the child and parent modules are inlined, and both the child and parent modules are multiply instantiated.
This results in multiple copies of the child module's body. If the body has references to some non-local annotations, depending on the inlining context only some of the hierarchical paths must be valid after inlining.

This PR maintains a set of hierarchical paths that are active based on an inlining context. The inlining context is defined as the sequence of `Instance` ops that the inlining algorithm is currently traversing. 
Any hierarchical path update is based on the set of active paths only. This filters out hierarchical paths that did not exist before inline.
